ogTitle: Napier Lane vs Napoleon Reef: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Napier Lane and Napoleon Reef.
heading: Napier Lane vs Napoleon Reef — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Napier Lane, New South Wales and Napoleon Reef, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Napoleon Reef has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Napier Lane (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Napier Lane or Napoleon Reef safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Napier Lane has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Napoleon Reef scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Napier Lane scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Napoleon Reef scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Napier Lane or Napoleon Reef?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Napoleon Reef has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.82% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
